title: So what exactly are those drawings in EoE
display_title: So what exactly are those drawings in EoE
wikitext:
[]: So what exactly are those drawings in EoE?             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Oct 28, 2007 4:16 pm There was a sequences full of what looked like... kid's drawings, and then more realistic drawings. One of them was the head of a fish, the other was a fish in a bucket, and then there was a fox that looked like it was dying. Oh, and a cat. What were they, and what did they mean?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Oct 28, 2007 4:19 pm Those were all drawn by people that hate Anno and Eva. Matt Greenfield said so.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Oct 28, 2007 4:22 pm What you're talking about is from EoE, when Shinji strangles Asuka and Third Impact occurs. You also see them in the Mindrape sequence of Episode 22'. After Asuka yells at Shinji in her mind for being "there" (her heart), she says off-screen "Nobody! Nobody! Nobody!" (while those images are shown on screen) and crying little Asuka says "So, look at me!" just before little Asuka/the 15th(?) asks Asuka if she's lonely. In terms of WHAT it means, I have no idea. I know I asked the same question in another thread. @ Brik-kun.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Oct 28, 2007 4:50 pm For what it's worth: From Episode 22' Looks like a greenhouse possibly? Maybe a cat and a table? Looks like 2 people who's been killed Someone who's throat has been cut? Looks like a husband and wife and a house Angry face? From EoE: Same angry face, looks like wearing glasses, maybe a beard too? Same image as first in the ep22' sequence Same image as third in the ep22' sequence, can see more here Same image as the fourth in the ep22' sequence Looks like a dog that's been gutted fish head with flies? Same image from the second in the ep22' sequence. Is the guy in black holding a knife? A marker drawn railroad crossing light A marker/pencil drawn sleeping cat.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Oct 28, 2007 4:57 pm Nice. I think the stoplight and cat appear in the real life sequence of somewhere in Japan, though I'm not sure. I don't know, when I look at the fish head and flies, I can't help think about Lord of the Flies.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Oct 28, 2007 5:01 pm Those drawings are creepy. But artistic.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Oct 28, 2007 5:13 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Those drawings are creepy. But artistic.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Oct 28, 2007 5:40 pm Barinax wrote: when I look at the fish head and flies, I can't help think about Lord of the Flies.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Oct 28, 2007 5:45 pm it's me, I'm the drawings in the EoE

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 28, 2007 6:19 pm I read somewhere (but have no source) that those pictures were drawn by actually mentally disturbed children. Those fish and cat pictures are pretty good; I'd like to meet that mentally disturbed child (though ve's certainly not a child any longer).

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sun Oct 28, 2007 6:24 pm I can only assume it's supposed to connote children who are slightly cuckoo, ie Shinji and Asuka. I am reminded of the Simpsons episode where Bart makes a creepy (and reminiscent of these) drawing entitled "SAD".

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Oct 28, 2007 6:53 pm I've heard somewhere that the pictures were drawn by kids; whether or not they had mental problems, I dunno. In terms of the angry face (with glasses and a bread), is it supposed to be Gendou or Anno??? (Both look alike, that's for sure!) I know the cat's on a fence in EoE's real-life sequence, I think it looked mostly the same.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Oct 28, 2007 9:19 pm That angry face looks like Anno, from the beard.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Mon Oct 29, 2007 9:35 am Szmitten wrote: I can only assume it's supposed to connote children who are slightly cuckoo, ie Shinji and Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Mon Oct 29, 2007 11:13 am Furthermore, it's a different cat. I know, that cat is my highlight of the whole live action sequence.

[]:             Quote Post by eva unit 04 » Mon Oct 29, 2007 1:19 pm the pic of the house and family looks like gendo and yui to me, and the greenhouse looking one looks like Kaji with his plants.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Oct 29, 2007 3:31 pm eva unit 04 wrote: the pic of the house and family looks like gendo and yui to me, and the greenhouse looking one looks like Kaji with his plants.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Mon Oct 29, 2007 3:37 pm The dog reminds me of Eva-02 in the movie and what these sucker harpies did to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Mon Oct 29, 2007 8:04 pm Ornette wrote: eva unit 04 wrote: the pic of the house and family looks like gendo and yui to me, and the greenhouse looking one looks like Kaji with his plants.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Oct 29, 2007 8:33 pm I PRESUMED they were to evoke feelings of childhood trauma....you know the movie trope of depicting those through poorly drawn child-level crayon drawings.....but what EXACTLY they were trying to get across....
summary:
[]: So what exactly are those drawings in EoE?             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Oct 28, 2007 4:16 pm There was a sequences full of what looked like... kid's drawings, and then more realistic drawings. One of them was the head of a fish, the other was a fish in a bucket, and then there was a fox that looked like it was dying. Oh, and a cat. What were they, and what did they mean?

[]:         &nb
